draft.introduction: Surah Al-Qadr ("The Night of Power") celebrates the night on which the Quran was first revealed. It declares this single night "better than a thousand months," making it the most spiritually potent moment in the Islamic calendar.

translation.text: Indeed, We sent it down on the Night of Power. And what will make you know what the Night of Power is? The Night of Power is better than a thousand months. The angels and the Spirit descend therein by the permission of their Lord for every matter. Peace it is, until the rising of the dawn.
translation.original_text: إِنَّا أَنزَلْنَاهُ فِي لَيْلَةِ الْقَدْرِ وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا لَيْلَةُ الْقَدْرِ

translator_notes.note: "The Spirit" (al-Ruh) is generally identified with the angel Gabriel, though some Sufi commentators distinguish it as a higher angelic being. The descent of angels "for every matter" suggests that on this night the divine decrees for the coming year are distributed—a concept linking Laylat al-Qadr to the preserved tablet (al-Lawh al-Mahfuz).
